description_long: This object consists of a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television set placed on top of a dark wooden cabinet. The television has a boxy, black and silver frame with a convex glass screen, which is smudged with visible handprints and partially covered by a sheet of newspaper. The front panel features a set of rotary dials and buttons for manual control, while the back reveals ventilation grilles, a power cord, and some exposed wiring. The wooden cabinet below the TV has a shelf containing scattered magazines and books, as well as a lower drawer with two metallic handles. The surfaces of both the TV and cabinet show signs of wear, with scratches and faded areas, suggesting age and frequent use.
description: A vintage CRT television with a smudged screen and manual controls sits atop a worn wooden cabinet with magazines and a drawer.
description_view_0: Front view showing the TV's convex screen with handprints, control dials, and the cabinet's open shelf with magazines and a drawer.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the TV's wooden side panel with chipped paint and the solid side of the cabinet.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the TV's ventilation grilles, power cord, exposed wiring, and the plain back of the cabinet.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the TV's other wooden side panel and the cabinet's unadorned side.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, black plastic bezel with a silver trim, rectangular and utilitarian with no decorative features.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and buttons on the lower right front panel.
